response:
The error you are encountering likely stems from how the session ID is being handled or transmitted to the TMDB API, as the platform requires a valid and active session ID that matches the user's authentication token. When you create a new session ID in the else block, there is a possibility that the API call to fetch account details is executed before the session is fully initialized or recognized by TMDB's servers, leading to an authentication failure. Additionally, if the session ID stored in local storage has expired or is invalid, relying on it without verifying its current status can also trigger this specific error.

To resolve this issue, you should ensure that the session creation process completes successfully before attempting to fetch user data, and implement error handling to catch any API failures gracefully. It is also advisable to validate the session ID's expiration status before using the one from local storage, and to refresh the session if it is no longer valid. By adding try-catch blocks around your API calls and checking the response status explicitly, you can prevent the application from dispatching invalid user data and provide clearer feedback when authentication fails.
